title: Pyramid of Meidum
description: The collapsed pyramid of Sneferu (formerly Huni), representing the transition from stepped to true pyramids. Located in Meidum Area, Old Kingdom.

title: Mortuary Temple of Meidum
description: The first example of a standard mortuary temple, located on the east side of the Meidum Pyramid. Meidum Area, 4th Dynasty.

title: Causeway of Meidum
description: Processional causeway cut into the bedrock, leading from the valley to the mortuary temple. Meidum Area, 4th Dynasty.

title: Mastaba 17 (The Unknown Mastaba)
description: Huge mudbrick mastaba north of the pyramid, featuring a robber's tunnel and granite sarcophagus. Meidum Area, 4th Dynasty.

title: Mastaba of Nefermaat and Atet (Mastaba 16)
description: Tomb of Prince Nefermaat and his wife Atet, where the famous "Geese of Meidum" painting was discovered. Meidum Area, 4th Dynasty.

title: Mastaba of Rahotep (Mastaba 6)
description: Tomb of Prince Rahotep and Nofret, famous for their lifelike seated statues (now in Cairo Museum). Meidum Area, 4th Dynasty.

title: Mastaba of Raderes
description: A smaller nobleman's mastaba located within the greater Meidum necropolis. Meidum Area, Old Kingdom.

title: Temple of Heryshef
description: The cult center of the ram-headed god Heryshef, expanded by Ramesses II. Herakleopolis Magna (Ehnasya el-Medina), New Kingdom.

title: Kom el-Eqareb Necropolis
description: Excavation area containing tombs and artifacts from the First Intermediate Period and Middle Kingdom. Herakleopolis Magna.

title: Roman Basilica Ruins
description: Remains of a large basilica with crushed red granite columns, signifying the city's Coptic importance. Herakleopolis Magna.

title: Colossal Statue of Ramesses II
description: Fallen limestone colossus of the pharaoh located near the Temple of Heryshef. Herakleopolis Magna.

title: Hypostyle Hall of Herakleopolis
description: Columned hall remains within the Heryshef temple precinct, featuring palmiform granite columns. Herakleopolis Magna.

title: First Pylon of Heryshef Temple
description: Ruins of the massive entrance gateway to the temple complex. Herakleopolis Magna.

title: Temple of Amun at El-Hiba
description: Temple built by Shoshenq I (22nd Dynasty) dedicated to Amun. El-Hiba (Teudjoi), Third Intermediate Period.

title: City Walls of Teudjoi
description: Limestone town walls and fortifications protecting the frontier city. El-Hiba, 21st-22nd Dynasty.

title: El-Hiba Necropolis
description: Hillside tombs containing sarcophagi and papyri (including the Story of Wenamun). El-Hiba, Third Intermediate/Late Period.

title: Tomb of Inti
description: Rock-cut tomb of a high official, featuring war scenes and daily life reliefs. Dishasha, 6th Dynasty (Old Kingdom).

title: Tomb of Shedu
description: Another major rock-cut tomb in the cliff face, belonging to the Iteti family. Dishasha, Old Kingdom.

title: Dishasha Rock Cut Tombs
description: Cliff necropolis overlooking the Bahr Yussef, containing burials of local nomarchs. Dishasha, Old/New Kingdom.

title: Sedment el-Gebel Necropolis
description: The main cemetery for Herakleopolis Magna, used from the First Intermediate Period to the New Kingdom. Sedment.

title: Tomb of Mery-Ra-Ha-Shetf
description: Famous tomb where wooden statues of the owner were found (now in British Museum). Sedment, 6th Dynasty.

title: New Kingdom Cemetery of Sedment
description: Section of the necropolis containing burials of wealth and status from the 18th and 19th Dynasties. Sedment.

title: Abusir el-Meleq Necropolis
description: Vast cemetery site with Hyksos era burials and Late Period mummies. Abusir el-Meleq.

title: Hyksos Graves
description: Burials attributed to the Hyksos period, offering insights into the Second Intermediate Period. Abusir el-Meleq.

title: Medinet Gurob (Kom Gurob)
description: Site of a New Kingdom town and royal harem palace established by Thutmose III. Gurob/Lahun entrance.

title: Palace of Amenhotep III
description: Ruins of the royal palace complex, known for finding objects of Queen Tiye. Medinet Gurob.

title: Temple of Thutmose III
description: Remains of a temple dedicated to the worship of Thutmose III. Medinet Gurob.

title: Monastery of the Virgin (Bayad el-Arab)
description: Historic monastery on the East Bank where the Holy Family is believed to have rested. Bayad el-Arab.

title: Monastery of St. Anthony (Deir el-Maymun)
description: The site where St. Anthony the Great lived in solitude before moving to the Red Sea. Deir el-Maymun, East Bank.

title: Church of Mar Girgis (Biba)
description: Ancient church containing relics of St. George and a stone associated with the Holy Family. Biba.

title: Mosque of Lady Hourya
description: Shrine and mosque of Sayyida Hourya, a descendant of Imam Hussein, known for its distinct architecture. Beni Suef City.

title: Mosque of El-Ghamri
description: A historic Mamluk-era style mosque and local landmark. Beni Suef City.

title: Cave of Sannur
description: A massive alabaster cave system in the Eastern Desert, a geological protectorate. Wadi Sannur.

title: Wadi Sannur Quarry
description: Ancient alabaster quarries used since pharaonic times. Wadi Sannur.

title: Gebel el-Nour
description: Limestone mountain on the East Bank with ancient quarries and Coptic inscriptions. Near Bayad el-Arab.

title: Kom el-Dabaa
description: Archaeological mound with stratified remains from various periods. Near Meidum.

title: Beni Suef Museum
description: Provincial museum housing artifacts from Ehnasya, Meidum, and Abusir el-Meleq. Beni Suef City.

title: Dallas (Ancient Nilo-Polis)
description: Site of the ancient city Nilo-Polis, featuring Roman and Coptic remains. Dallas village.

title: Ruins of Byzantine Houses
description: Domestic architecture remnants from the Byzantine era at Herakleopolis. Ehnasya el-Medina.

title: Kom Isfaht (Apollonopolis Parva)
description: Ancient Greco-Roman town site, located near Abusir el-Meleq.

title: Fortress of El-Hiba
description: Specific military fortifications overlooking the Nile to control river traffic. El-Hiba.
